sentences: Dayavan: Truth is Power (English: "The Compassionate") is a 1988 Hindi thriller movie remake of the Tamil film "Nayagan" (1987).
The movie was directed by Feroz Khan, and starred Vinod Khanna, Feroz Khan and Madhuri Dixit in lead roles with Aditya Pancholi in an important role.
Girish Kohli (born November 29, 1983 in Mumbai) is an Indian author and screenwriter.
He is the writer of the Hindi thriller movie MOM.
Dharmatama is a 1975 Hindi thriller movie and the first Bollywood film to be shot in Afghanistan.
It was produced and directed by Feroz Khan.
The movie is the first attempt in India to localise "The Godfather".
The title character was based on matka (form of Indian gambling) king Ratan Khatri.
It is said that Khan sat down with Khatri to learn more about him and understand the nuances of matka.
The cast includes Feroz Khan, Hema Malini, Rekha, Premnath, Imtiaz Khan, Danny Denzongpa, Farida Jalal, Ranjeet, Helen, Madan Puri, Jeevan, Iftekhar and Dara Singh, satyen kappu and sudhir.
The music is by Kalyanji Anandji.
This film took Feroz Khan to new heights in his career as this was a blockbuster movie.
The same year, "Sholay" and "Deewar" were released.
The film is also its scenes featuring Buzkashi a Central Asian sport on horses, including aerial shots, which in turn won film's cinematographer, Kamal Bose, Filmfare Award for Best Cinematographer.

Kites is a 2010 Indian Hindi/Spanish mix language dialogue romantic action thriller film directed by Anurag Basu, story written and produced by Rakesh Roshan, starring Hrithik Roshan, Bárbara Mori, Kangana Ranaut, and Kabir Bedi.
The film was released in India and in North America on 21 May 2010.
Its 208-theater opening in North America made it the largest Bollywood release up to that time.
It was also the first Bollywood movie to reach the weekend top ten, though "My Name is Khan" had a larger first-weekend North American gross.
Reliance Entertainment bought worldwide distribution rights of Kites for whooping () , which is a record sum .
Despite a strong opening, The film only managed to collect () net in its lifetime run following a critical loss.
The film had been aired in a week of its release on smallscreen .

A betting pool, sports lottery, sweep, or office pool if done at work, is a form of gambling, specifically a variant of parimutuel betting influenced by lotteries, where gamblers pay a fixed price into a "pool" (from which taxes and a house "take" or "vig" are removed), and then make a selection on an outcome, usually related to sport.
In an informal game, the vig is usually quite small or non-existent.
The pool is evenly divided between those that have made the correct selection.
There are no odds involved; each winner's payoff depends simply on the number of gamblers and the number of winners.
(True parimutuel betting, which was historically referred to as pool betting, involves both odds calculations and variable wager amounts.)
Khoon Bhari Maang (English: Blood on the Head) is an Indian Hindi thriller movie released on 12 August 1988, directed and produced by Rakesh Roshan.
It is a remake of the Australian mini-series "Return to Eden" (1983), and is about a wealthy widow who is almost killed by her lover and sets out for revenge.
The film was a comeback venture for Rekha and was an critical and commercial success.
The film was remade in Telugu as "Gowthami" starring Suhasini.
Lion of Gujarat is a 2015 Bollywood action drama directed by Dinesh Lamba and produced by Jitendra Gohil.

This is a first Bollywood film shot entirely in Gujarat starring Dinesh Lamba, Homi Wadia and Akruti Agrawal in lead roles and Milan Vaidya, Manoj Parmar, Arman Tahil, Uday Danger, Yashpal, Gazal Rai, Bharat Thakkar in supporting role.
The move was shot in the locations of Jamnagar, Porbandar and other parts of Gujarat.
"Lion of Gujarat" is scheduled for release on 26 June 2015.
"Aama" (आमा) (Literally: Mother) is the first Nepali movie produced in Nepal, starring Shiva Shankar Manandhar and Bhuvan Chand (Thapa) as the leading actors.
The movie was produced by the Information Department of the Nepalese Government and released on October 7, 1964.
Bollywood film maker Hira Singh Khatri was invited by the late King Mahendra to direct the first Nepali movie.
Baaz: A Bird in Danger is 2003 Hindi thriller movie directed by Tinnu Verma and starring Sunil Shetty, Karisma Kapoor, Jackie Shroff.
Other cast members include Dino Morea, Preeti Jhangiani Aditi Gowitrikar and Suhasini Mulay.
Matka" gambling or satta" is a form of lottery which originally involved betting on the opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange.
It originates from before the era of Indian independence when it was known as "Ankada Jugar" ("figures gambling").
In the 1960s, the system was replaced with other ways of generating random numbers, including pulling slips from a large earthenware pot known as a "matka", or dealing playing cards.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

hop1_docs: Matka gambling | Matka" gambling or satta" is a form of lottery which originally involved betting on the opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange.  It originates from before the era of Indian independence when it was known as "Ankada Jugar" ("figures gambling").  In the 1960s, the system was replaced with other ways of generating random numbers, including pulling slips from a large earthenware pot known as a "matka", or dealing playing cards.

Dharmatma | Dharmatama is a 1975 Hindi thriller movie and the first Bollywood film to be shot in Afghanistan.  It was produced and directed by Feroz Khan.  The movie is the first attempt in India to localise "The Godfather".  The title character was based on matka (form of Indian gambling) king Ratan Khatri.  It is said that Khan sat down with Khatri to learn more about him and understand the nuances of matka.  The cast includes Feroz Khan, Hema Malini, Rekha, Premnath, Imtiaz Khan, Danny Denzongpa, Farida Jalal, Ranjeet, Helen, Madan Puri, Jeevan, Iftekhar and Dara Singh, satyen kappu and sudhir.  The music is by Kalyanji Anandji.  This film took Feroz Khan to new heights in his career as this was a blockbuster movie.  The same year, "Sholay" and "Deewar" were released.  The film is also its scenes featuring Buzkashi a Central Asian sport on horses, including aerial shots, which in turn won film's cinematographer, Kamal Bose, Filmfare Award for Best Cinematographer.

Betting pool | A betting pool, sports lottery, sweep, or office pool if done at work, is a form of gambling, specifically a variant of parimutuel betting influenced by lotteries, where gamblers pay a fixed price into a "pool" (from which taxes and a house "take" or "vig" are removed), and then make a selection on an outcome, usually related to sport.  In an informal game, the vig is usually quite small or non-existent.  The pool is evenly divided between those that have made the correct selection.  There are no odds involved; each winner's payoff depends simply on the number of gamblers and the number of winners.  (True parimutuel betting, which was historically referred to as pool betting, involves both odds calculations and variable wager amounts.)

Matka Joanna | Matka Joanna is an album by Polish jazz trumpeter and composer Tomasz Stańko recorded in 1994 and released on the ECM label.  The music was inspired by Jerzy Kawalerowicz's 1961 movie "Matka Joanna od Aniołów".

Naya Zaher | Naya Zaher is a Hindi drama cum thriller movie of Bollywood directed by Jyoti Sarup and music direction made by Kalyanji–Anandji.  This movie was released in 1991 and it was India's first Hindi feature film based on HIV/AIDS.  The mode has given of an adult thriller, where the main villain in the film is not a person, but the disease, AIDS.

Lottery betting | Lottery betting is the activity of predicting the results of a lottery draw and placing a wager on the outcome.  Lottery betting (also known as lotto betting) is a form of online gambling, run by licensed betting firms, where players place bets on the outcome of lottery draws instead of buying physical or online  tickets via official lottery operators.

Matka Canyon | Matka (Macedonian: Матка , meaning "womb") is a canyon located west of central Skopje, Macedonia.  Covering roughly 5,000 hectares, Matka is one of the most popular outdoor destinations in Macedonia and is home to several medieval monasteries.  The Matka Lake within the Matka Canyon is the oldest artificial lake in the country.

- Matka gambling / source: passage [1] «Matka gambling | Matka" gambling or satta" ...» (quote: "opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange") — [1] «Matka gambling».

- Spelling/label variant: question uses "Dharmatama" but passage label is "Dharmatma" ([2]) — treat both as candidate film pages.
- "Matka" appears in other, unrelated entries in the passages (e.g., [4] «Matka Joanna», [7] «Matka Canyon»); ensure the retrieval targets the gambling sense (passage [1]) rather than unrelated "Matka" pages.
- No separate Ratan Khatri page is present in the supplied passages (only mentioned within [2]).

3) Bridge entity / Missing fact:
- Bridge entity (as written in passages): "matka (form of Indian gambling)" and "Ratan Khatri" (both in [2]) and the page title "Matka gambling" ([1]).
- Precise missing fact to confirm: authoritative verification that the cotton opening/closing rates were transmitted "from the New York Cotton Exchange" (i.e., confirm the transmission source and historical phrasing on primary pages).

4) Retrieval direction (prioritized):
- Open "Matka gambling" page (lede/Origin/History sections) to confirm the quoted line about "opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange." Search keywords: Matka gambling, "opening and closing rates" "New York Cotton Exchange".
- Open "New York Cotton Exchange" page (lede/History) to verify that it transmitted cotton rates and the historical channel/name used.
- Open "Dharmatma" (or "Dharmatama") page (lede/Production/Cultural impact) to confirm the film's link to matka and Ratan Khatri; check for exact wording and source citation.
- Open "Ratan Khatri" page or disambiguation (if exists) and check biography/coverage for his role in matka and any mention of source of rates.

Final answer deferred until those pages/sections are retrieved and their statements/citations are verified.
response.hop2_query: Matka gambling opening and closing rates New York Cotton Exchange
hop2_docs: Matka gambling | Matka" gambling or satta" is a form of lottery which originally involved betting on the opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange.  It originates from before the era of Indian independence when it was known as "Ankada Jugar" ("figures gambling").  In the 1960s, the system was replaced with other ways of generating random numbers, including pulling slips from a large earthenware pot known as a "matka", or dealing playing cards.

New York Cotton Exchange | The New York Cotton Exchange (NYCE) is a commodities exchange founded in 1870 by a group of one hundred cotton brokers and merchants at 1 Hanover Square in New York City.  In 1998, the New York Board of Trade (NYBOT) became the parent company of both the New York Cotton Exchange, and it is now owned by IntercontinentalExchange (ICE).  Since 2003, its headquarters and trading facility have been in the New York Mercantile Exchange Building.

New Orleans Cotton Exchange | The New Orleans Cotton Exchange was established in New Orleans, Louisiana, in 1871 as a centralized forum for the trade of cotton.  It operated in New Orleans until closing in 1964.  Occupying several buildings over its history, its final location, the New Orleans Cotton Exchange Building, is now a National Historic Landmark.

Mobile Cotton Exchange | The Mobile Cotton Exchange was a commodities exchange that operated from 1871 until 1942 in the Alabama port city of Mobile to enable key local cotton factors and merchants to maintain control over cotton sales, warehousing, and shipping from Mobile Bay.  It was the third cotton exchange founded in the United States, following those in New York and New Orleans.  The exchange in Mobile was followed by exchanges in Savannah and Memphis.

Coffee, Sugar and Cocoa Exchange | The Coffee, Sugar and Cocoa Exchange (CSCE) was founded in 1882 as the Coffee Exchange in the City of New York.  Sugar futures were added in 1914, and, on September 28, 1979, the New York Coffee and Sugar Exchange merged with the New York Cocoa Exchange (which in turn had been founded in 1925) to form CSCE.  In 1998, CSCE merged with the New York Cotton Exchange as subsidiaries of the New York Board of Trade (NYBOT).  The CSCE operates as an independent unit of NYBOT trading futures and options on coffee, sugar and cocoa and the S&P Commodity Index.  Trading is by open outcry, from 8 a.m. to 2:45 p.m., Monday through Friday.  In January 2007, NYBOT merged with IntercontinentalExchange (ICE) and became a wholly owned subsidiary of ICE.

New York Gold Exchange | The New York Gold Exchange was an exchange formed shortly after the beginning of the American Civil War for the purpose of creating an open market for transactions involving gold and the government-created paper currency, the greenback.  Established in 1862, it closed in 1897.

New-York Open Board of Stock Brokers | The New-York Open Board of Stock Brokers was a stock exchange in New York City.  Shortly after being formed, the New-York Open Board of Stock Brokers held their first annual election on February 21, 1877.  87 votes were cast at Delmonico's on New Street.  B. M. Nevers was elected president.  E. T. Bragaw, former vice president of the New-York Gold Exchange was elected chairman.  The building of the old Open Board of Stock Brokers was commissioned as the new permanent location of the exchange, with an initial membership of 272 and memberships pending.
hop2_titles: New-York Open Board of Stock Brokers
response.summary_2: opening and closing rates of cotton transmitted from the New York Cotton Exchange. New York Cotton Exchange is a commodities exchange founded in 1870 in New York City.
